description: Register scheduling.find_entry v1 as a non-side-effectful workflow action with descriptive input/output schemas.
Implement scheduling.find_entry tenant-scoped lookup with assigned_user_ids and normalized schedule entry output.
Apply private-entry redaction policy in scheduling.find_entry according to the confirmed visibility rule.
Register scheduling.search_entries v1 as a non-side-effectful workflow action with bounded search inputs and normalized list output.
Implement scheduling.search_entries filters for time window, assigned users, work item, status, text query, and limit.
Require user_schedule:read for read-side scheduling actions using the workflow run actor and tenant transaction helper.
Register scheduling.reschedule v1 as a side-effectful workflow action with engine-provided idempotency.
Implement reschedule input validation for entry id, ISO start/end, start-before-end, conflict mode, recurrence scope, reason, and note.
Implement reusable conflict detection for assigned users that excludes the target entry/series and ignores canceled/completed/no-show entries.
Implement reschedule conflict_mode=fail returning a standard CONFLICT workflow action error with safe conflict details.
Implement reschedule conflict_mode=shift preserving duration and moving to the earliest non-conflicting slot after detected conflicts.
Implement reschedule conflict_mode=override and create unresolved schedule_conflicts records for overlapping entries.
Apply reschedule recurrence_scope using existing single/future/all recurrence semantics where possible.
Emit APPOINTMENT_RESCHEDULED for appointment-like reschedule mutations and return previous/new time output fields.
Register scheduling.reassign v1 as a side-effectful workflow action with assignment mode and no-op controls.
Implement technician eligibility validation for reassign using active/internal user checks and the confirmed Technician-role rule.
Implement reassign mode=replace and mode=add against schedule_entry_assignees with unique assigned_user_ids.
Implement reassign no_op_if_already_assigned behavior that returns changed=false and suppresses assignment events when effective assignments are unchanged.
Apply reassign recurrence_scope using existing recurrence update semantics where possible.
Emit APPOINTMENT_ASSIGNED according to the confirmed single- or multi-assignee event policy and return previous/new assignee outputs.
Register scheduling.cancel v1 as a side-effectful workflow action with recurrence scope, reason, and note inputs.
Implement cancel by marking the selected entry or recurrence scope canceled without deleting the schedule entry row/series.
Emit APPOINTMENT_CANCELED for appointment-like canceled entries and include optional reason.
Register scheduling.complete v1 as a side-effectful workflow action with optional outcome/note inputs.
Implement complete by marking the selected entry or recurrence scope completed and returning completion metadata.
Emit APPOINTMENT_COMPLETED for appointment-like completed entries and include optional outcome.
Write workflow run audit records for each write action with action id/version, entry id, actor, recurrence scope, changed data, and reason/note metadata.
Standardize scheduling action errors through throwActionError for validation, not found, permission, conflict, transient, and internal failures.
Ensure all new scheduling action ids appear in the workflow designer Scheduling group without additional catalog wiring.

Document final decisions for multi-assignee event behavior, cancel permission, event-publish failure behavior, private-entry visibility, and assign_user event scope in the plan scratchpad before implementation is marked complete.
Update shared workflow action actor resolution to respect tenant-owned workflow_definitions when resolving the workflow run actor for permission checks.

Mirror the newer Client workflow action test/metadata patterns for scheduling actions, including picker metadata conversion checks, runtime catalog grouping, and best-effort lazy workflow event publication.
